Orc Impaler  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: RANGE PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 23
DMG: 7 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +3 WIS: +4 HP: +10
WT: 3.0 Size: SMALL
Class: RNG
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)
Orc Impaler
Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: RANGE PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 23
DMG: 7 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +3 WIS: +4 HP: +10
WT: 3.0 Size: SMALL
Class: RNG
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Type:1H Piercing
Stackable:No
Submitted By:iventheassassin
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-17 03:18:55
Page Updated:Tue Jan 27th, 2026

Expansion: Original
Rarity: Uncommon
Level to Attain: 35
[Given | Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
The Orc Impaler

Obtained From

This item is obtained from NPCs.

Lesser Faydark
NPC Name Expansion
Bryn Fynndel Original

# Oct 19 2003 at 12:51 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362292728
63 posts
Ew... I understand why it would be piercing.. considering you would be impaling (which is a lovely consept itself.. bleh) someone (namely an orc) but.. range?

I dont want to know how you would do that from a distance...

Maybe throw it in the air and hope it lands straight down on-

/shivers

(If you are wondering why I bothered... think about it in a roleplay mode)

674 posts
Friends friends FRIENDS!!! sheeesh. first of all, your a ranger. you will use your bow when your ready for range. it should look distintly enough you should not throw it away? In MOST cases you put items in range to utilize their stats and when you outgrow them. Most are very valuable for that reason. but yes, it would be a pain in the butt for a ranger. Maybe they should have made it a bow? lol.

i aggree on the wishful bane damage.

Finally got the Shadowman Leader to spawn. I went through over a dozen PH's. The spawn point mentioned isn't the only place he spawns OR he ran away too fast to agro on my sitting toons right there. Sometimes I'd check at timer and he'd be attacking. Anyway if anyone else tries this quest be prepared to spend a day or two camping for this guy.

I couldnt get over this guy Groth Human pally comon im not here to flame but if you dont know what your talking about then dont talk cause only thing coming out of your mouth is BS warriors using bow more then rangers damn when did that start to happen yeah sure they use bows but not more then rangers i use my bow every raid with disc i can pump out over 30k dAMAGE in matter of seconds and without disc im shooting for like 700-1500damage shots but back to the warriors using bow more then rangers thats totally BS if you ever been on a real raid you would know that mostly the warrior just runs up to a bunch of mobs and brings em all to the raid and lets people stomp em unless your doing a nice level 70 mob then you got your warrior and the CH cycle but even mobs level 70 ive been MT for on many raids when the MT wasnt on that night so unless you know what your talking about dont post it in the end everyone is a badass with a uber guild and uber equipment i got like 600-800hp lower then a 65 pal/sk maybe 1300lower then a 65 war but hey there not many who can whoop my *** so untill you start raiding and know what your talking about dont post you killing naggy with your same lame friends every week is hardly a raid at all.http://www.magelo.com/eq_view_profile.html?num=710555

Groth man, you have obviously never even ventured in to the world of norrath as a Ranger. Ranger's are absolutley DEADLY with a bow. Their is NO other class that is as skilled with a Bow as a ranger is. Yeah, other classes can USE a bow, but we are MASTERS with it.

QuoteReply
# Jul 23 2002 at 3:40 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591780539252
59 posts
So...Rangers use bows more often at lower levels as compared to higher levels? Do you even know what a high level Ranger can do with a good bow in his hands? Apparently not judging from you're post. Did it ever occur to you that BOWS can have stats too? Put your account back on ebay where it belongs...
▲ Page top
RE: listen rangers geez
QuoteReply
# Jun 02 2002 at 9:26 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592254715371
48 posts
Are you kidding me? Its actually the exact opposite of your first statement. Ahem...

1) Rangers are the only class that can critical hit with bows, and even though you can at low levels, a lvl 5 ranger probably wont have a very high dex. At higher levels, you will have a higher dex, scoring more crits, scoring more damage.

2) At lvl 55(high lvl) rangers get the Trueshot Disc. One of the most useful discs in the game. You use your BOW, not your weps for this disc. EXTREMELY useful on raids, tons of damage dealt.

3) There's a thing called GOOD bows, usually attainable at higher lvls... not lower levels. If you think that some newbie bow would be better for a low level than a great bow for a high level, your smokin something...

4) Another thing good to know in EQ: AA points. Rangers get class specific points centered around archery. Endless Quiver, infinite amount for WHATEVER arrow is in your range slot and innate bow haste. Yes that means that you can have an infinite amount of Gloomwater arrows, 10 150 and its LORE... or one of the higher Luclin fletched items I don't know about. Archery Mastery, increasing your max bow damage up to 2 times with 3 points in the skill.

Please... if your not a ranger and don't truly know about the usefulness of bows in the life of a ranger, then don't get mad at other people that know. Sure if your in direct combat, you would probably switch your bow out for stats... but other than that, bow is the way to go.

Peace rangers
▲ Page top
RE: listen rangers geez
QuoteReply
# Sep 23 2002 at 10:51 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591779456764
49 posts
errrm Actually, Rangers aren't the only class that can crit with bows, they only get crits with bows, not melee, but my warrior crits with her bow quite often.
▲ Page top
RE: listen rangers geez
QuoteReply
# Jun 09 2003 at 10:49 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591767079068
Sage
266 posts
Okumabear is correct. My warrior has gotten critical hits with his bow.

However, you are right on the other points. The pally who started this topic is basically wrong on all accounts.

My warrior has a great bow, with stats (twisted longbow) that he fires ONE time, maybe two per fight, as he's pulling the mob.

A Ranger on the other hand, can stand back from the fight, and pump arrows into the mob while I tank it, for massive DPS.

I've seen the damage done by my guild's 63 ranger, with Endless Quiver, A good bow and arrow, and disc up. Its insane. A warrior can't, and shouldn't even try, to fight this way.

The warrior is there to breathe in the face of the mob, smack the snot out of it, and keep it looking at him, while the ranger fills the mob with arrows (and of course the other damage types do their job).

Ranger + bow = awesome damage
Warrior+ bow = puller. One shot then melee, maybe a second shot if the mob runs slow :)

Edited, Mon Jun 9 11:09:30 2003
▲ Page top
RE: listen rangers geez
QuoteReply

__DEL__1592759473426
9 posts

Rangers can hit for 1500ish with their bows at higher levels... and they almost never melee. Why don't you shut your "neb" paladin yapper.

__DEL__1592757520881
31 posts
You need to learn piercing so you can use a dragon horn ykesha(yeah i know its just 1 weapon, but its fricking nice) plus its always good to have the option to use piercing weapons, and it only takes a couple hours in a group that allows you to practice it.
▲ Page top
RE: why?
QuoteReply
# Aug 29 2001 at 1:22 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592247010486
Scholar
360 posts
I agree. Rangers (and the other dual wield classes) have a HUGE advantage when it comes to one handed weapon training. You guessed it: stick the trainer in the offhand.

Yes, I know that skill advances much slower this way. The point is, it DOES advance, and you can still be a reasonably productive member of the group while you train. Or, if you're in a known/guild group, you can either put the trainer in the mainhand, OR equip 2 trainers for fastest advancement.

I know - nothing revolutionary. But, just pointing it out to the younger Rangers out there who want to train an alternate weapon skill (which they should) after lvl 17. Just be sure to keep the 2H skills up as well; they're not as easy to train, and you'll need them later on.

Arynduil Greyleaf
35th Ranger of Tunare
EMarr

RE: 1H or 2h?
QuoteReply
# Jul 05 2001 at 7:35 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591752191797
Scholar
198 posts
First off it is a one handed weapon.

Second, Verant seems to have moved away from the 1hs weapons being the only great weapons since Kunark and Velious came out so keeping piercing skill up is always a good thing for a ranger, or any melee class, not to mention that the stat boost of this weapon makes it something handy to keep in your range slot when you aren't using your bow; Just make sure to not throw it at a mob.

as for a ranger keeping piercing up..
i know i don't.
there arent many items that i would look for that are piercing. Most are low dmg, low dly.
Now, as for it being rng only and for rng slot..
Switching out this and a bow is horrible. You might accidently throw it when you think you have your bow in. besides that, 4 wis, 3 str, and 10 hp isnt the kind of thing i would put so much work into.

just my 2cp
▲ Page top
RE: 1H or 2h?
QuoteReply
# Aug 07 2001 at 11:38 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762304683
3 posts
I have to disagree there. There are acutally quite a few nice piercing weapons for rangers. To name a few would be this one, for mid-lower an Icy Blade, also a Dragon Horn Yak which has better ration than regular Yak and still procs,The Lupine Dagger is 9/18, +4 Dex, +4 Wis, and procs Greater Wolf Form. And lastly im just nameing a few here is the Fayguard Parrying Dagger. It is 9/17 10AC Procs Word of Pain. Imagine if your lvl 45 or around there, get one of these and your piercing skill was still 0. Man that would suck lol.

Kingheavyj
Lvl 27 Ranger
Proud member of Stormwarden
The Tribunal

Why? The reason why is the Dragonhorn Ykesha. It is faster tan the regular yak and procs 1 lvl sooner, and guess what, its ranger only.

ok the whole point of this weapon is to keep it in ranged slot for stats, also there is a ranger only piercing weapon that is pretty darm good as well as a couple 2hb staffs out there that no good ranger should be without so it is up to you.
good luck

I keep seeing all you 'higher level' rangers saying the point of this is to keep it in your range slot for stats, Are you all STUPID??? I don't think your even rangers, Um hey ever heard of a BOW??? yea my bow goes in my range slot... I'm a ranger, rangers use bow's. The whole point of this weapon? nothing it sucks like most questable weapons do before lvl 45. get it if you want to use a semidecent piercer... range slot, your fools not rangers, pshaw

Maeniel Wolfkin
24th season
▲ Page top
RE: Why?
QuoteReply
# Sep 20 2005 at 1:06 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593967664270
2 posts
I haven't played in a good few years, so I don't know if the game has drastically changed since I last had the pleasure (if it was even a pleasure then), but when I played you could drag weapon slots, ranged slots, backpacks, and basically every other thing onto your hotkey menu. This makes is extremely easy to switch out, say, a bow and an Orc Impaler from the time you're at range to the time you're in melee combat. I used to use this setup so that 3 simple clicks equipped my Swarmcaller for the slow proc, and then reequip my dual wield for damage. You should think about things a bit harder before you call them stupid or useless.

Stetharus Respius
53 Wood Elf Ranger
Erollisi Marr
Long Retired

Hello.

I was trying to get rancorous ghasts to spawn in lfay today. While killing skellies at the obelisk the ghoul boss spawned. Some friends finished him off. He spawned again not 15 minutes later right on the obelisks. He did drop the log book. Too bad no rangers were around :(. Anyway, he is out there...good luck. (at least on Povar he is :)

I got lucky in finding the Boss up at the undead rune, now i'm trying to get the Shadowed Man Leader. He does wander, sort of, on a set track through a little vally right beside the north zone wall, between the Gfay zone and the brownie village, PH is a normal Shadowed Man.

Ok, after reading a post from a reliable source, the best way is to camp the ghoul boss first, just sit and kill everything at the ruins and he will eventually spawn.

Then go after the shadowman leader

# Apr 02 2001 at 10:50 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I can not confirm any of this, some posts have stated that the shadowman leader is a wandering mob as well and that none of the shadow men at the tower are his placeholder.

I also read a post stating that the ghoul boss will not spawn until you kill the shadowman leader and turn in the item to the NPC.

Then this triggers the Ghoul boss to spawn.

▲ Page top
RE: Getting spawns
QuoteReply
# Aug 29 2001 at 1:33 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247010486
Scholar
360 posts
All evidence points to both of these MoBs being random spawns with placeholders. The well know PH for the Shadowman Leader is the wandering shadowman along the north wall (watch out for Brownie Scouts - i'd recommend pulling to the GFay zone area). The Ghoul Boss is just a rare spawn at the Undead Generator. Keep killing everything and he should pop, eventually. People have hinted that Brownies dont wander near the Undead Ruins. I can neither confirm or deny this.

Arynduil Greyleaf
35th Ranger of Tunare
EMarr
